driver#

Data types used by CUDA driver#

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUuuid_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
.. attribute:: bytes

   < CUDA definition of UUID

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemFabricHandle_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Fabric handle - An opaque handle representing a memory allocation
that can be exported to processes in same or different nodes. For
IPC between processes on different nodes they must be connected via
the NVSwitch fabric.

.. attribute:: data



   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUipcEventHandle_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
CUDA IPC event handle

.. attribute:: reserved



   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUipcMemHandle_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
CUDA IPC mem handle

.. attribute:: reserved



   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamBatchMemOpParams_union(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Per-operation parameters for cuStreamBatchMemOp

.. attribute:: operation

   Operation. This is the first field of all the union elemets and
   acts as a TAG to determine which union member is valid.

   :type: CUstreamBatchMemOpType

.. attribute:: waitValue

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_32 and
   CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_64 operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpWaitValueParams_st

.. attribute:: writeValue

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_32 and
   CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_64 operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpWriteValueParams_st

.. attribute:: flushRemoteWrites

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpFlushRemoteWritesParams_st

.. attribute:: memoryBarrier

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_BARRIER operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpMemoryBarrierParams_st

.. attribute:: atomicReduction



   :type: CUstreamMemOpAtomicReductionParams_st

.. attribute:: pad



   :type: list[cuuint64_t]

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_BATCH_MEM_OP_NODE_PARAMS_v1_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Batch memory operation node parameters  Used in the legacy
cuGraphAddBatchMemOpNode api. New code should use cuGraphAddNode()

.. attribute:: ctx



   :type: CUcontext

.. attribute:: count



   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: paramArray



   :type: CUstreamBatchMemOpParams

.. attribute:: flags



   :type: unsigned int

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_BATCH_MEM_OP_NODE_PARAMS_v2_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Batch memory operation node parameters

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context to use for the operations.

   :type: CUcontext

.. attribute:: count

   Number of operations in paramArray.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: paramArray

   Array of batch memory operations.

   :type: CUstreamBatchMemOpParams

.. attribute:: flags

   Flags to control the node.

   :type: unsigned int

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUasyncNotificationInfo_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Information passed to the user via the async notification callback

.. attribute:: type

   The type of notification being sent

   :type: CUasyncNotificationType

.. attribute:: info

   Information about the notification. `typename` must be checked in
   order to interpret this field.

   :type: anon_union2

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUdevprop_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Legacy device properties

.. attribute:: maxThreadsPerBlock

   Maximum number of threads per block

   :type: int

.. attribute:: maxThreadsDim

   Maximum size of each dimension of a block

   :type: list[int]

.. attribute:: maxGridSize

   Maximum size of each dimension of a grid

   :type: list[int]

.. attribute:: sharedMemPerBlock

   Shared memory available per block in bytes

   :type: int

.. attribute:: totalConstantMemory

   Constant memory available on device in bytes

   :type: int

.. attribute:: SIMDWidth

   Warp size in threads

   :type: int

.. attribute:: memPitch

   Maximum pitch in bytes allowed by memory copies

   :type: int

.. attribute:: regsPerBlock

   32-bit registers available per block

   :type: int

.. attribute:: clockRate

   Clock frequency in kilohertz

   :type: int

.. attribute:: textureAlign

   Alignment requirement for textures

   :type: int

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUaccessPolicyWindow_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Specifies an access policy for a window, a contiguous extent of
memory beginning at base_ptr and ending at base_ptr + num_bytes.
num_bytes is limited by
CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAX_ACCESS_POLICY_WINDOW_SIZE. Partition into
many segments and assign segments such that: sum of "hit segments"
/ window == approx. ratio. sum of "miss segments" / window ==
approx 1-ratio. Segments and ratio specifications are fitted to the
capabilities of the architecture. Accesses in a hit segment apply
the hitProp access policy. Accesses in a miss segment apply the
missProp access policy.

.. attribute:: base_ptr

   Starting address of the access policy window. CUDA driver may align
   it.

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: num_bytes

   Size in bytes of the window policy. CUDA driver may restrict the
   maximum size and alignment.

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: hitRatio

   hitRatio specifies percentage of lines assigned hitProp, rest are
   assigned missProp.

   :type: float

.. attribute:: hitProp

   CUaccessProperty set for hit.

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. attribute:: missProp

   CUaccessProperty set for miss. Must be either NORMAL or STREAMING

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
GPU kernel node parameters

.. attribute:: func

   Kernel to launch

   :type: CUfunction

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: kernelParams

   Array of pointers to kernel parameters

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: extra

   Extra options

   :type: Any

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS_v2_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
GPU kernel node parameters

.. attribute:: func

   Kernel to launch

   :type: CUfunction

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: kernelParams

   Array of pointers to kernel parameters

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: extra

   Extra options

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: kern

   Kernel to launch, will only be referenced if func is NULL

   :type: CUkernel

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context for the kernel task to run in. The value NULL will indicate
   the current context should be used by the api. This field is
   ignored if func is set.

   :type: CUcontext

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS_v3_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
GPU kernel node parameters

.. attribute:: func

   Kernel to launch

   :type: CUfunction

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: kernelParams

   Array of pointers to kernel parameters

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: extra

   Extra options

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: kern

   Kernel to launch, will only be referenced if func is NULL

   :type: CUkernel

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context for the kernel task to run in. The value NULL will indicate
   the current context should be used by the api. This field is
   ignored if func is set.

   :type: CUcontext

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

.. attribute:: value

.. attribute:: elementSize

.. attribute:: width

   Width of the row in elements

.. attribute:: height

   Number of rows

   :type: size_t

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_CONDITIONAL_NODE_PARAMS(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Conditional node parameters

.. attribute:: handle

   Conditional node handle. Handles must be created in advance of
   creating the node using cuGraphConditionalHandleCreate.

   :type: CUgraphConditionalHandle

.. attribute:: type

   Type of conditional node.

   :type: CUgraphConditionalNodeType

.. attribute:: size

   Size of graph output array. Allowed values are 1 for
   CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_WHILE, 1 or 2 for CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_IF, or any
   value greater than zero for CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_SWITCH.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: phGraph_out

   CUDA-owned array populated with conditional node child graphs
   during creation of the node. Valid for the lifetime of the
   conditional node. The contents of the graph(s) are subject to the
   following constraints:   - Allowed node types are kernel nodes,
   empty nodes, child graphs, memsets, memcopies, and conditionals.
   This applies recursively to child graphs and conditional bodies.
   - All kernels, including kernels in nested conditionals or child
   graphs at any level, must belong to the same CUDA context.
   These graphs may be populated using graph node creation APIs or
   cuStreamBeginCaptureToGraph.  CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_IF: phGraph_out[0]
   is executed when the condition is non-zero. If `size` == 2,
   phGraph_out[1] will be executed when the condition is zero.
   CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_WHILE: phGraph_out[0] is executed as long as the
   condition is non-zero. CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_SWITCH: phGraph_out[n] is
   executed when the condition is equal to n. If the condition >=
   `size`, no body graph is executed.

   :type: CUgraph

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context on which to run the node. Must match context used to create
   the handle and all body nodes.

   :type: CUcontext

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphEdgeData_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Optional annotation for edges in a CUDA graph. Note, all edges
implicitly have annotations and default to a zero-initialized value
if not specified. A zero-initialized struct indicates a standard
full serialization of two nodes with memory visibility.

.. attribute:: from_port

   This indicates when the dependency is triggered from the upstream
   node on the edge. The meaning is specfic to the node type. A value
   of 0 in all cases means full completion of the upstream node, with
   memory visibility to the downstream node or portion thereof
   (indicated by `to_port`).   Only kernel nodes define non-zero
   ports. A kernel node can use the following output port types:
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_DEFAULT,
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_PROGRAMMATIC, or
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_LAUNCH_ORDER.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: to_port

   This indicates what portion of the downstream node is dependent on
   the upstream node or portion thereof (indicated by `from_port`).
   The meaning is specific to the node type. A value of 0 in all cases
   means the entirety of the downstream node is dependent on the
   upstream work.   Currently no node types define non-zero ports.
   Accordingly, this field must be set to zero.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: type

   This should be populated with a value from CUgraphDependencyType.
   (It is typed as char due to compiler-specific layout of bitfields.)
   See CUgraphDependencyType.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: reserved

   These bytes are unused and must be zeroed. This ensures
   compatibility if additional fields are added in the future.

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_PARAMS_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Graph instantiation parameters

.. attribute:: flags

   Instantiation flags

   :type: cuuint64_t

.. attribute:: hUploadStream

   Upload stream

   :type: CUstream

.. attribute:: hErrNode_out

   The node which caused instantiation to fail, if any

   :type: CUgraphNode

.. attribute:: result_out

   Whether instantiation was successful. If it failed, the reason why

   :type: CUgraphInstantiateResult

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Memory Synchronization Domain map  See ::cudaLaunchMemSyncDomain.
By default, kernels are launched in domain 0. Kernel launched with
CU_LAUNCH_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_REMOTE will have a different domain ID.
User may also alter the domain ID with CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap for
a specific stream / graph node / kernel launch. See
CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_MAP.  Domain ID range is
available through CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_COUNT.

.. attribute:: default_

   The default domain ID to use for designated kernels

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: remote

   The remote domain ID to use for designated kernels

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchAttributeValue_union(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Launch attributes union; used as value field of CUlaunchAttribute

.. attribute:: pad



   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: accessPolicyWindow

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_POLICY_WINDOW.

   :type: CUaccessPolicyWindow

.. attribute:: cooperative

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE. Nonzero
   indicates a cooperative kernel (see cuLaunchCooperativeKernel).

   :type: int

.. attribute:: syncPolicy

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_SYNCHRONIZATION_POLICY. CUsynchronizationPolicy
   for work queued up in this stream

   :type: CUsynchronizationPolicy

.. attribute:: clusterDim

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION
   that represents the desired cluster dimensions for the kernel.
   Opaque type with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of
   the cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension.
   - `y` - The Y dimension of the cluster, in blocks. Must be a
   divisor of the grid Y dimension.    - `z` - The Z dimension of the
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid Z dimension.

   :type: anon_struct1

.. attribute:: clusterSchedulingPolicyPreference

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_SCHEDULING_POLICY_PREFERENCE. Cluster
   scheduling policy preference for the kernel.

   :type: CUclusterSchedulingPolicy

.. attribute:: programmaticStreamSerializationAllowed

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PROGRAMMATIC_STREAM_SERIALIZATION.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: programmaticEvent

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PROGRAMMATIC_EVENT
   with the following fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when
   all blocks trigger it.    - `Event` record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept :CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.
   - `triggerAtBlockStart` - If this is set to non-0, each block
   launch will automatically trigger the event.

   :type: anon_struct2

.. attribute:: launchCompletionEvent

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_LAUNCH_COMPLETION_EVENT with the following
   fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when the last block
   launches    - `int` flags; - Event record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.

   :type: anon_struct3

.. attribute:: priority

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PRIORITY. Execution
   priority of the kernel.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: memSyncDomainMap

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_MAP.
   See CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap.

   :type: CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap

.. attribute:: memSyncDomain

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN.
   See::CUlaunchMemSyncDomain

   :type: CUlaunchMemSyncDomain

.. attribute:: preferredClusterDim

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_CLUSTER_DIMENSION that represents the
   desired preferred cluster dimensions for the kernel. Opaque type
   with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of the preferred
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension, and
   must be a multiple of the `x` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `y` - The Y dimension of
   the preferred cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid Y
   dimension, and must be a multiple of the `y` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `z` - The Z dimension of
   the preferred cluster, in blocks. Must be equal to the `z` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.

   :type: anon_struct4

.. attribute:: deviceUpdatableKernelNode

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_DEVICE_UPDATABLE_KERNEL_NODE. with the
   following fields: - `int` deviceUpdatable - Whether or not the
   resulting kernel node should be device-updatable.    -
   `CUgraphDeviceNode` devNode - Returns a handle to pass to the
   various device-side update functions.

   :type: anon_struct5

.. attribute:: sharedMemCarveout

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_SHARED_MEMORY_CARVEOUT.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: nvlinkUtilCentricScheduling



   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: portableClusterSizeMode

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PORTABLE_CLUSTER_SIZE_MODE.

   :type: CUlaunchAttributePortableClusterMode

.. attribute:: sharedMemoryMode

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_SHARED_MEMORY_MODE.
   See CUsharedMemoryMode for acceptable values.

   :type: CUsharedMemoryMode

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUctxCreateParams_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Params for creating CUDA context. Both execAffinityParams and
cigParams cannot be non-NULL at the same time. If both are NULL,
the context will be created as a regular CUDA context.

.. attribute:: execAffinityParams

   Array of execution affinity parameters to limit context resources
   (e.g., SM count). Only supported Volta+ MPS. Mutually exclusive
   with cigParams.

   :type: CUexecAffinityParam

.. attribute:: numExecAffinityParams

   Number of elements in execAffinityParams array. Must be 0 if
   execAffinityParams is NULL.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: cigParams

   CIG (CUDA in Graphics) parameters for sharing data from
   D3D12/Vulkan graphics clients. Mutually exclusive with
   execAffinityParams.

   :type: CUctxCigParam

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_SIGNAL_PARAMS_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
External semaphore signal parameters

.. attribute:: params



   :type: anon_struct16

.. attribute:: flags

   Only when CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_SIGNAL_PARAMS is used to signal a
   CUexternalSemaphore of type
   CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCISYNC, the valid flag is
   CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_SIGNAL_SKIP_NVSCIBUF_MEMSYNC which
   indicates that while signaling the CUexternalSemaphore, no memory
   synchronization operations should be performed for any external
   memory object imported as CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCIBUF.
   For all other types of CUexternalSemaphore, flags must be zero.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: reserved



   :type: list[unsigned int]

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_WAIT_PARAMS_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
External semaphore wait parameters

.. attribute:: params



   :type: anon_struct19

.. attribute:: flags

   Only when CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_WAIT_PARAMS is used to wait on a
   CUexternalSemaphore of type
   CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCISYNC, the valid flag is
   CUDA_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_WAIT_SKIP_NVSCIBUF_MEMSYNC which indicates
   that while waiting for the CUexternalSemaphore, no memory
   synchronization operations should be performed for any external
   memory object imported as CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCIBUF.
   For all other types of CUexternalSemaphore, flags must be zero.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: reserved



   :type: list[unsigned int]

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphExecUpdateResultInfo_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Result information returned by cuGraphExecUpdate

.. attribute:: result

   Gives more specific detail when a cuda graph update fails.

   :type: CUgraphExecUpdateResult

.. attribute:: errorNode

   The "to node" of the error edge when the topologies do not match.
   The error node when the error is associated with a specific node.
   NULL when the error is generic.

   :type: CUgraphNode

.. attribute:: errorFromNode

   The from node of error edge when the topologies do not match.
   Otherwise NULL.

   :type: CUgraphNode

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemPoolProps_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Specifies the properties of allocations made from the pool.

.. attribute:: allocType

   Allocation type. Currently must be specified as
   CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_PINNED

   :type: CUmemAllocationType

.. attribute:: handleTypes

   Handle types that will be supported by allocations from the pool.

   :type: CUmemAllocationHandleType

.. attribute:: location

   Location where allocations should reside.

   :type: CUmemLocation

.. attribute:: win32SecurityAttributes

   Windows-specific LPSECURITYATTRIBUTES required when
   CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_WIN32 is specified. This security attribute
   defines the scope of which exported allocations may be transferred
   to other processes. In all other cases, this field is required to
   be zero.

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: maxSize

   Maximum pool size. When set to 0, defaults to a system dependent
   value.

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: usage

   Bitmask indicating intended usage for the pool.

   :type: unsigned short

.. attribute:: reserved

   reserved for future use, must be 0

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemcpyAttributes_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Attributes specific to copies within a batch. For more details on
usage see cuMemcpyBatchAsync.

.. attribute:: srcAccessOrder

   Source access ordering to be observed for copies with this
   attribute.

   :type: CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder

.. attribute:: srcLocHint

   Hint location for the source operand. Ignored when the pointers are
   not managed memory or memory allocated outside CUDA.

   :type: CUmemLocation

.. attribute:: dstLocHint

   Hint location for the destination operand. Ignored when the
   pointers are not managed memory or memory allocated outside CUDA.

   :type: CUmemLocation

.. attribute:: flags

   Additional flags for copies with this attribute. See CUmemcpyFlags

   :type: unsigned int

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_MEM_ALLOC_NODE_PARAMS_v1_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Memory allocation node parameters

.. attribute:: poolProps

   in: location where the allocation should reside (specified in
   ::location). ::handleTypes must be CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_NONE. IPC is
   not supported.

   :type: CUmemPoolProps

.. attribute:: accessDescs

   in: array of memory access descriptors. Used to describe peer GPU
   access

   :type: CUmemAccessDesc

.. attribute:: accessDescCount

   in: number of memory access descriptors. Must not exceed the number
   of GPUs.

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: bytesize

   in: size in bytes of the requested allocation

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: dptr

   out: address of the allocation returned by CUDA

   :type: CUdeviceptr

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_CHILD_GRAPH_NODE_PARAMS_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Child graph node parameters

.. attribute:: graph

   The child graph to clone into the node for node creation, or a
   handle to the graph owned by the node for node query. The graph
   must not contain conditional nodes. Graphs containing memory
   allocation or memory free nodes must set the ownership to be moved
   to the parent.

   :type: CUgraph

.. attribute:: ownership

   The ownership relationship of the child graph node.

   :type: CUgraphChildGraphNodeOwnership

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUeglFrame_st(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
CUDA EGLFrame structure Descriptor - structure defining one frame
of EGL.  Each frame may contain one or more planes depending on
whether the surface * is Multiplanar or not.

   :type: anon_union16

.. attribute:: width

   Width of first plane

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: height

   Height of first plane

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: depth

   Depth of first plane

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: pitch

   Pitch of first plane

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: planeCount

   Number of planes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: numChannels

   Number of channels for the plane

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: frameType

   Array or Pitch

   :type: CUeglFrameType

.. attribute:: eglColorFormat

   CUDA EGL Color Format

   :type: CUeglColorFormat

.. attribute:: cuFormat

   CUDA Array Format

   :type: CUarray_format

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUipcMem_flags(value: int)#
CUDA Ipc Mem Flags
CU_IPC_MEM_LAZY_ENABLE_PEER_ACCESS = <CUipcMem_flags.CU_IPC_MEM_LAZY_ENABLE_PEER_ACCESS: 1>#

Automatically enable peer access between remote devices as needed

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAttach_flags(value: int)#
CUDA Mem Attach Flags
CU_MEM_ATTACH_GLOBAL = <CUmemAttach_flags.CU_MEM_ATTACH_GLOBAL: 1>#

Memory can be accessed by any stream on any device

CU_MEM_ATTACH_HOST = <CUmemAttach_flags.CU_MEM_ATTACH_HOST: 2>#

Memory cannot be accessed by any stream on any device

CU_MEM_ATTACH_SINGLE = <CUmemAttach_flags.CU_MEM_ATTACH_SINGLE: 4>#

Memory can only be accessed by a single stream on the associated device

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUctx_flags(value: int)#
Context creation flags
CU_CTX_SCHED_AUTO = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SCHED_AUTO: 0>#

Automatic scheduling

CU_CTX_SCHED_SPIN = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SCHED_SPIN: 1>#

Set spin as default scheduling

CU_CTX_SCHED_YIELD = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SCHED_YIELD: 2>#

Set yield as default scheduling

CU_CTX_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC: 4>#

Set blocking synchronization as default scheduling

CU_CTX_BLOCKING_SYNC = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_BLOCKING_SYNC: 4>#

Set blocking synchronization as default scheduling [Deprecated]

CU_CTX_SCHED_MASK = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SCHED_MASK: 7>#
CU_CTX_MAP_HOST = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_MAP_HOST: 8>#

[Deprecated]

CU_CTX_LMEM_RESIZE_TO_MAX = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_LMEM_RESIZE_TO_MAX: 16>#

Keep local memory allocation after launch

CU_CTX_COREDUMP_ENABLE = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_COREDUMP_ENABLE: 32>#

Trigger coredumps from exceptions in this context

CU_CTX_USER_COREDUMP_ENABLE = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_USER_COREDUMP_ENABLE: 64>#

Enable user pipe to trigger coredumps in this context

CU_CTX_SYNC_MEMOPS = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_SYNC_MEMOPS: 128>#

Ensure synchronous memory operations on this context will synchronize

CU_CTX_FLAGS_MASK = <CUctx_flags.CU_CTX_FLAGS_MASK: 255>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUevent_sched_flags(value: int)#
Event sched flags
CU_EVENT_SCHED_AUTO = <CUevent_sched_flags.CU_EVENT_SCHED_AUTO: 0>#

Automatic scheduling

CU_EVENT_SCHED_SPIN = <CUevent_sched_flags.CU_EVENT_SCHED_SPIN: 1>#

Set spin as default scheduling

CU_EVENT_SCHED_YIELD = <CUevent_sched_flags.CU_EVENT_SCHED_YIELD: 2>#

Set yield as default scheduling

CU_EVENT_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC = <CUevent_sched_flags.CU_EVENT_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC: 4>#

Set blocking synchronization as default scheduling

class cuda.bindings.driver.cl_event_flags(value: int)#
NVCL event scheduling flags
NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_AUTO = <cl_event_flags.NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_AUTO: 0>#

Automatic scheduling

NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_SPIN = <cl_event_flags.NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_SPIN: 1>#

Set spin as default scheduling

NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_YIELD = <cl_event_flags.NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_YIELD: 2>#

Set yield as default scheduling

NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC = <cl_event_flags.NVCL_EVENT_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC: 4>#

Set blocking synchronization as default scheduling

class cuda.bindings.driver.cl_context_flags(value: int)#
NVCL context scheduling flags
NVCL_CTX_SCHED_AUTO = <cl_context_flags.NVCL_CTX_SCHED_AUTO: 0>#

Automatic scheduling

NVCL_CTX_SCHED_SPIN = <cl_context_flags.NVCL_CTX_SCHED_SPIN: 1>#

Set spin as default scheduling

NVCL_CTX_SCHED_YIELD = <cl_context_flags.NVCL_CTX_SCHED_YIELD: 2>#

Set yield as default scheduling

NVCL_CTX_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC = <cl_context_flags.NVCL_CTX_SCHED_BLOCKING_SYNC: 4>#

Set blocking synchronization as default scheduling

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUhostTaskSyncMode(value: int)#
CU_HOST_TASK_BLOCKING = <CUhostTaskSyncMode.CU_HOST_TASK_BLOCKING: 0>#

The execution thread will block until new host tasks are ready to run

CU_HOST_TASK_SPINWAIT = <CUhostTaskSyncMode.CU_HOST_TASK_SPINWAIT: 1>#

The execution thread will spin wait until new host tasks are ready to run

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstream_flags(value: int)#
Stream creation flags
CU_STREAM_DEFAULT = <CUstream_flags.CU_STREAM_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default stream flag

CU_STREAM_NON_BLOCKING = <CUstream_flags.CU_STREAM_NON_BLOCKING: 1>#

Stream does not synchronize with stream 0 (the NULL stream)

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUevent_flags(value: int)#
Event creation flags
CU_EVENT_DEFAULT = <CUevent_flags.CU_EVENT_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default event flag

CU_EVENT_BLOCKING_SYNC = <CUevent_flags.CU_EVENT_BLOCKING_SYNC: 1>#

Event uses blocking synchronization

CU_EVENT_DISABLE_TIMING = <CUevent_flags.CU_EVENT_DISABLE_TIMING: 2>#

Event will not record timing data

CU_EVENT_INTERPROCESS = <CUevent_flags.CU_EVENT_INTERPROCESS: 4>#

Event is suitable for interprocess use. CU_EVENT_DISABLE_TIMING must be set

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUevent_record_flags(value: int)#
Event record flags
CU_EVENT_RECORD_DEFAULT = <CUevent_record_flags.CU_EVENT_RECORD_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default event record flag

CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL = <CUevent_record_flags.CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL: 1>#

When using stream capture, create an event record node instead of the default behavior. This flag is invalid when used outside of capture.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUevent_wait_flags(value: int)#
Event wait flags
CU_EVENT_WAIT_DEFAULT = <CUevent_wait_flags.CU_EVENT_WAIT_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default event wait flag

CU_EVENT_WAIT_EXTERNAL = <CUevent_wait_flags.CU_EVENT_WAIT_EXTERNAL: 1>#

When using stream capture, create an event wait node instead of the default behavior. This flag is invalid when used outside of capture.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUatomicOperation(value: int)#
CUDA-valid Atomic Operations
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_ADD = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_ADD: 0>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_MIN = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_MIN: 1>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_MAX = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_MAX: 2>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_INCREMENT = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_INCREMENT: 3>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_DECREMENT = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_INTEGER_DECREMENT: 4>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_AND = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_AND: 5>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_OR = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_OR: 6>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_XOR = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_XOR: 7>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_EXCHANGE = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_EXCHANGE: 8>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_CAS = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_CAS: 9>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_ADD = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_ADD: 10>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_MIN = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_MIN: 11>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_MAX = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_FLOAT_MAX: 12>#
CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_MAX = <CUatomicOperation.CU_ATOMIC_OPERATION_MAX: 13>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUatomicOperationCapability(value: int)#
CUDA-valid Atomic Operation capabilities
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamWaitValue_flags(value: int)#
Flags for :py:obj:`~.cuStreamWaitValue32` and
:py:obj:`~.cuStreamWaitValue64`
CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_GEQ = <CUstreamWaitValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_GEQ: 0>#

Wait until (int32_t)(*addr - value) >= 0 (or int64_t for 64 bit values). Note this is a cyclic comparison which ignores wraparound. (Default behavior.)

CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_EQ = <CUstreamWaitValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_EQ: 1>#

Wait until *addr == value.

CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_AND = <CUstreamWaitValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_AND: 2>#

Wait until (*addr & value) != 0.

CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_NOR = <CUstreamWaitValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_NOR: 3>#

Wait until ~(*addr | value) != 0. Support for this operation can be queried with cuDeviceGetAttribute() and CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_CAN_USE_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_NOR.

CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_FLUSH = <CUstreamWaitValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_FLUSH: 1073741824>#

Follow the wait operation with a flush of outstanding remote writes. This means that, if a remote write operation is guaranteed to have reached the device before the wait can be satisfied, that write is guaranteed to be visible to downstream device work. The device is permitted to reorder remote writes internally. For example, this flag would be required if two remote writes arrive in a defined order, the wait is satisfied by the second write, and downstream work needs to observe the first write. Support for this operation is restricted to selected platforms and can be queried with CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_CAN_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamWriteValue_flags(value: int)#
Flags for :py:obj:`~.cuStreamWriteValue32`
CU_STREAM_WRITE_VALUE_DEFAULT = <CUstreamWriteValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WRITE_VALUE_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default behavior

CU_STREAM_WRITE_VALUE_NO_MEMORY_BARRIER = <CUstreamWriteValue_flags.CU_STREAM_WRITE_VALUE_NO_MEMORY_BARRIER: 1>#

Permits the write to be reordered with writes which were issued before it, as a performance optimization. Normally, cuStreamWriteValue32 will provide a memory fence before the write, which has similar semantics to __threadfence_system() but is scoped to the stream rather than a CUDA thread. This flag is not supported in the v2 API.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamBatchMemOpType(value: int)#
Operations for :py:obj:`~.cuStreamBatchMemOp`
CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_32 = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_32: 1>#

Represents a cuStreamWaitValue32 operation

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_32 = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_32: 2>#

Represents a cuStreamWriteValue32 operation

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_64 = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_64: 4>#

Represents a cuStreamWaitValue64 operation

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_64 = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_64: 5>#

Represents a cuStreamWriteValue64 operation

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_BARRIER = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_BARRIER: 6>#

Insert a memory barrier of the specified type

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_ATOMIC_REDUCTION = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_ATOMIC_REDUCTION: 8>#

Perform a atomic reduction. See atomicReduction

CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES = <CUstreamBatchMemOpType.CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES: 3>#

This has the same effect as CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_FLUSH, but as a standalone operation.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamMemoryBarrier_flags(value: int)#
Flags for :py:obj:`~.CUstreamBatchMemOpParams.memoryBarrier`
CU_STREAM_MEMORY_BARRIER_TYPE_SYS = <CUstreamMemoryBarrier_flags.CU_STREAM_MEMORY_BARRIER_TYPE_SYS: 0>#

System-wide memory barrier.

CU_STREAM_MEMORY_BARRIER_TYPE_GPU = <CUstreamMemoryBarrier_flags.CU_STREAM_MEMORY_BARRIER_TYPE_GPU: 1>#

Limit memory barrier scope to the GPU.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamAtomicReductionOpType(value: int)#
Atomic reduction operation types for
:py:obj:`~.CUstreamBatchMemOpParams`::atomicReduction::reductionOp
CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_OR = <CUstreamAtomicReductionOpType.CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_OR: 6>#

Performs an atomic OR: *(address) = *(address) | value

CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_AND = <CUstreamAtomicReductionOpType.CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_AND: 5>#

Performs an atomic AND: *(address) = *(address) & value

CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_ADD = <CUstreamAtomicReductionOpType.CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_OP_ADD: 0>#

Performs an atomic ADD: *(address) = *(address) + value

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamAtomicReductionDataType(value: int)#
Atomic reduction data types for
:py:obj:`~.CUstreamBatchMemOpParams`::atomicReduction::dataType
CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_UNSIGNED_32 = <CUstreamAtomicReductionDataType.CU_STREAM_ATOMIC_REDUCTION_UNSIGNED_32: 14>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUoccupancy_flags(value: int)#
Occupancy calculator flag
CU_OCCUPANCY_DEFAULT = <CUoccupancy_flags.CU_OCCUPANCY_DEFAULT: 0>#

Default behavior

CU_OCCUPANCY_DISABLE_CACHING_OVERRIDE = <CUoccupancy_flags.CU_OCCUPANCY_DISABLE_CACHING_OVERRIDE: 1>#

Assume global caching is enabled and cannot be automatically turned off

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamUpdateCaptureDependencies_flags(value: int)#
Flags for :py:obj:`~.cuStreamUpdateCaptureDependencies`
CU_STREAM_ADD_CAPTURE_DEPENDENCIES = <CUstreamUpdateCaptureDependencies_flags.CU_STREAM_ADD_CAPTURE_DEPENDENCIES: 0>#

Add new nodes to the dependency set

CU_STREAM_SET_CAPTURE_DEPENDENCIES = <CUstreamUpdateCaptureDependencies_flags.CU_STREAM_SET_CAPTURE_DEPENDENCIES: 1>#

Replace the dependency set with the new nodes

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUasyncNotificationType(value: int)#
Types of async notification that can be sent
CU_ASYNC_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_OVER_BUDGET = <CUasyncNotificationType.CU_ASYNC_NOTIFICATION_TYPE_OVER_BUDGET: 1>#

Sent when the process has exceeded its device memory budget

Unsigned 8-bit integers

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNSIGNED_INT16 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNSIGNED_INT16: 2>#

Unsigned 16-bit integers

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNSIGNED_INT32 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNSIGNED_INT32: 3>#

Unsigned 32-bit integers

Signed 8-bit integers

Signed 16-bit integers

Signed 32-bit integers

16-bit floating point

32-bit floating point

CU_AD_FORMAT_NV12 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_NV12: 176>#

8-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:0 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X1 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X1: 192>#

1 channel unsigned 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X2: 193>#

2 channel unsigned 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X4 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT8X4: 194>#

4 channel unsigned 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X1 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X1: 195>#

1 channel unsigned 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X2: 196>#

2 channel unsigned 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X4 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT16X4: 197>#

4 channel unsigned 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X1 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X1: 198>#

1 channel signed 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X2: 199>#

2 channel signed 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X4 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT8X4: 200>#

4 channel signed 8-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X1 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X1: 201>#

1 channel signed 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X2: 202>#

2 channel signed 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X4 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_SNORM_INT16X4: 203>#

4 channel signed 16-bit normalized integer

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC1_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC1_UNORM: 145>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC1 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC1_UNORM_SRGB = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC1_UNORM_SRGB: 146>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC1 compression) format with sRGB encoding

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC2_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC2_UNORM: 147>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC2 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC2_UNORM_SRGB = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC2_UNORM_SRGB: 148>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC2 compression) format with sRGB encoding

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC3_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC3_UNORM: 149>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC3 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC3_UNORM_SRGB = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC3_UNORM_SRGB: 150>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC3 compression) format with sRGB encoding

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC4_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC4_UNORM: 151>#

1 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC4 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC4_SNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC4_SNORM: 152>#

1 channel signed normalized block-compressed (BC4 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC5_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC5_UNORM: 153>#

2 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC5 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC5_SNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC5_SNORM: 154>#

2 channel signed normalized block-compressed (BC5 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC6H_UF16 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC6H_UF16: 155>#

3 channel unsigned half-float block-compressed (BC6H compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC6H_SF16 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC6H_SF16: 156>#

3 channel signed half-float block-compressed (BC6H compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC7_UNORM = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC7_UNORM: 157>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC7 compression) format

CU_AD_FORMAT_BC7_UNORM_SRGB = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_BC7_UNORM_SRGB: 158>#

4 channel unsigned normalized block-compressed (BC7 compression) format with sRGB encoding

CU_AD_FORMAT_P010 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_P010: 159>#

10-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:0 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_P016 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_P016: 161>#

16-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:0 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_NV16 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_NV16: 162>#

8-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_P210 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_P210: 163>#

10-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_P216 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_P216: 164>#

16-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_YUY2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_YUY2: 165>#

2 channel, 8-bit YUV packed planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y210 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y210: 166>#

2 channel, 10-bit YUV packed planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y216 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y216: 167>#

2 channel, 16-bit YUV packed planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_AYUV = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_AYUV: 168>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y410 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y410: 169>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y416 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y416: 177>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y444_PLANAR8 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y444_PLANAR8: 178>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_Y444_PLANAR10 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_Y444_PLANAR10: 179>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_YUV444_8bit_SemiPlanar = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_YUV444_8bit_SemiPlanar: 180>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_YUV444_16bit_SemiPlanar = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_YUV444_16bit_SemiPlanar: 181>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT_101010_2 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UNORM_INT_101010_2: 80>#

4 channel unorm R10G10B10A2 RGB format

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PACKED_422 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PACKED_422: 81>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PACKED_444 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PACKED_444: 82>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_SEMIPLANAR_420 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_SEMIPLANAR_420: 83>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_SEMIPLANAR_444 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_SEMIPLANAR_444: 87>#

3 channel unsigned 8-bit YUV semi-planar format, with 4:4:4 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_SEMIPLANAR_444 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_SEMIPLANAR_444: 88>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_420 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_420: 90>#

3 channel unsigned 16-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:0 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PLANAR_422 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT8_PLANAR_422: 91>#

3 channel unsigned 8-bit YUV planar format, with 4:2:2 sampling

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_422 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_422: 92>#

CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_444 = <CUarray_format.CU_AD_FORMAT_UINT16_PLANAR_444: 94>#

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUaddress_mode(value: int)#
Texture reference addressing modes
CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_WRAP = <CUaddress_mode.CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_WRAP: 0>#

Wrapping address mode

CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_CLAMP = <CUaddress_mode.CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_CLAMP: 1>#

Clamp to edge address mode

CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_MIRROR = <CUaddress_mode.CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_MIRROR: 2>#

Mirror address mode

CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_BORDER = <CUaddress_mode.CU_TR_ADDRESS_MODE_BORDER: 3>#

Border address mode
