class cuda.bindings.driver.CUtensorMapFloatOOBfill(value: int)#
Tensor map out-of-bounds fill type
CU_TENSOR_MAP_FLOAT_OOB_FILL_NONE = <CUtensorMapFloatOOBfill.CU_TENSOR_MAP_FLOAT_OOB_FILL_NONE: 0>#
CU_TENSOR_MAP_FLOAT_OOB_FILL_NAN_REQUEST_ZERO_FMA = <CUtensorMapFloatOOBfill.CU_TENSOR_MAP_FLOAT_OOB_FILL_NAN_REQUEST_ZERO_FMA: 1>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUtensorMapIm2ColWideMode(value: int)#
Tensor map Im2Col wide mode
CU_TENSOR_MAP_IM2COL_WIDE_MODE_W = <CUtensorMapIm2ColWideMode.CU_TENSOR_MAP_IM2COL_WIDE_MODE_W: 0>#
CU_TENSOR_MAP_IM2COL_WIDE_MODE_W128 = <CUtensorMapIm2ColWideMode.CU_TENSOR_MAP_IM2COL_WIDE_MODE_W128: 1>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAGS(value: int)#
Access flags that specify the level of access the current context's
device has on the memory referenced.
CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_NONE = <CUDA_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAGS.CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_NONE: 0>#

No access, meaning the device cannot access this memory at all, thus must be staged through accessible memory in order to complete certain operations

CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_READ = <CUDA_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAGS.CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_READ: 1>#

Read-only access, meaning writes to this memory are considered invalid accesses and thus return error in that case.

CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_READWRITE = <CUDA_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAGS.CU_POINTER_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_FLAG_READWRITE: 3>#

Read-write access, the device has full read-write access to the memory

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUexternalMemoryHandleType(value: int)#
External memory handle types
CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_FD = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_FD: 1>#

Handle is an opaque file descriptor

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32 = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32: 2>#

Handle is an opaque shared NT handle

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32_KMT = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32_KMT: 3>#

Handle is an opaque, globally shared handle

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_HEAP = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_HEAP: 4>#

Handle is a D3D12 heap object

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_RESOURCE = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_RESOURCE: 5>#

Handle is a D3D12 committed resource

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_RESOURCE = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_RESOURCE: 6>#

Handle is a shared NT handle to a D3D11 resource

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_RESOURCE_KMT = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_RESOURCE_KMT: 7>#

Handle is a globally shared handle to a D3D11 resource

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCIBUF = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCIBUF: 8>#

Handle is an NvSciBuf object

CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_DMABUF_FD = <CUexternalMemoryHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_MEMORY_HANDLE_TYPE_DMABUF_FD: 9>#

Handle is a dma_buf file descriptor

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType(value: int)#
External semaphore handle types
CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_FD = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_FD: 1>#

Handle is an opaque file descriptor

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32 = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32: 2>#

Handle is an opaque shared NT handle

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32_KMT = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_OPAQUE_WIN32_KMT: 3>#

Handle is an opaque, globally shared handle

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_FENCE = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D12_FENCE: 4>#

Handle is a shared NT handle referencing a D3D12 fence object

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_FENCE = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_FENCE: 5>#

Handle is a shared NT handle referencing a D3D11 fence object

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCISYNC = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_NVSCISYNC: 6>#

Opaque handle to NvSciSync Object

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_KEYED_MUTEX = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_KEYED_MUTEX: 7>#

Handle is a shared NT handle referencing a D3D11 keyed mutex object

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_KEYED_MUTEX_KMT = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_D3D11_KEYED_MUTEX_KMT: 8>#

Handle is a globally shared handle referencing a D3D11 keyed mutex object

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORE_FD = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORE_FD: 9>#

Handle is an opaque file descriptor referencing a timeline semaphore

CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORE_WIN32 = <CUexternalSemaphoreHandleType.CU_EXTERNAL_SEMAPHORE_HANDLE_TYPE_TIMELINE_SEMAPHORE_WIN32: 10>#

Handle is an opaque shared NT handle referencing a timeline semaphore

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAllocationHandleType(value: int)#
Flags for specifying particular handle types
CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_NONE = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_NONE: 0>#

Does not allow any export mechanism. >

CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_POSIX_FILE_DESCRIPTOR = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_POSIX_FILE_DESCRIPTOR: 1>#

Allows a file descriptor to be used for exporting. Permitted only on POSIX systems. (int)

CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_WIN32 = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_WIN32: 2>#

Allows a Win32 NT handle to be used for exporting. (HANDLE)

CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_WIN32_KMT = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_WIN32_KMT: 4>#

Allows a Win32 KMT handle to be used for exporting. (D3DKMT_HANDLE)

CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_FABRIC = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_FABRIC: 8>#

Allows a fabric handle to be used for exporting. (CUmemFabricHandle)

CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_MAX = <CUmemAllocationHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAccess_flags(value: int)#
Specifies the memory protection flags for mapping.
CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_NONE = <CUmemAccess_flags.CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_NONE: 0>#

Default, make the address range not accessible

CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_READ = <CUmemAccess_flags.CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_READ: 1>#

Make the address range read accessible

CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_READWRITE = <CUmemAccess_flags.CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_READWRITE: 3>#

Make the address range read-write accessible

CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_MAX = <CUmemAccess_flags.CU_MEM_ACCESS_FLAGS_PROT_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemLocationType(value: int)#
Specifies the type of location
CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVALID = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVALID: 0>#
CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_NONE = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_NONE: 0>#

Location is unspecified. This is used when creating a managed memory pool to indicate no preferred location for the pool

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_DEVICE = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_DEVICE: 1>#

Location is a device location, thus id is a device ordinal

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST: 2>#

Location is host, id is ignored

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST_NUMA = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST_NUMA: 3>#

Location is a host NUMA node, thus id is a host NUMA node id

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST_NUMA_CURRENT = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_HOST_NUMA_CURRENT: 4>#

Location is a host NUMA node of the current thread, id is ignored

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVISIBLE = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVISIBLE: 5>#

Location is not visible but device is accessible, id is always CU_DEVICE_INVALID

CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_MAX = <CUmemLocationType.CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAllocationType(value: int)#
Defines the allocation types available
CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_INVALID = <CUmemAllocationType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_INVALID: 0>#
CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_PINNED = <CUmemAllocationType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_PINNED: 1>#

This allocation type is ‘pinned’, i.e. cannot migrate from its current location while the application is actively using it

CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_MANAGED = <CUmemAllocationType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_MANAGED: 2>#

This allocation type is managed memory

CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_MAX = <CUmemAllocationType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAllocationGranularity_flags(value: int)#
Flag for requesting different optimal and required granularities
for an allocation.
CU_MEM_ALLOC_GRANULARITY_MINIMUM = <CUmemAllocationGranularity_flags.CU_MEM_ALLOC_GRANULARITY_MINIMUM: 0>#

Minimum required granularity for allocation

CU_MEM_ALLOC_GRANULARITY_RECOMMENDED = <CUmemAllocationGranularity_flags.CU_MEM_ALLOC_GRANULARITY_RECOMMENDED: 1>#

Recommended granularity for allocation for best performance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemRangeHandleType(value: int)#
Specifies the handle type for address range
CU_MEM_RANGE_HANDLE_TYPE_DMA_BUF_FD = <CUmemRangeHandleType.CU_MEM_RANGE_HANDLE_TYPE_DMA_BUF_FD: 1>#
CU_MEM_RANGE_HANDLE_TYPE_MAX = <CUmemRangeHandleType.CU_MEM_RANGE_HANDLE_TYPE_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemRangeFlags(value: int)#
Flag for requesting handle type for address range.
CU_MEM_RANGE_FLAG_DMA_BUF_MAPPING_TYPE_PCIE = <CUmemRangeFlags.CU_MEM_RANGE_FLAG_DMA_BUF_MAPPING_TYPE_PCIE: 1>#

Indicates that DMA_BUF handle should be mapped via PCIe BAR1

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUarraySparseSubresourceType(value: int)#
Sparse subresource types
CU_ARRAY_SPARSE_SUBRESOURCE_TYPE_SPARSE_LEVEL = <CUarraySparseSubresourceType.CU_ARRAY_SPARSE_SUBRESOURCE_TYPE_SPARSE_LEVEL: 0>#
CU_ARRAY_SPARSE_SUBRESOURCE_TYPE_MIPTAIL = <CUarraySparseSubresourceType.CU_ARRAY_SPARSE_SUBRESOURCE_TYPE_MIPTAIL: 1>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemOperationType(value: int)#
Memory operation types
CU_MEM_OPERATION_TYPE_MAP = <CUmemOperationType.CU_MEM_OPERATION_TYPE_MAP: 1>#
CU_MEM_OPERATION_TYPE_UNMAP = <CUmemOperationType.CU_MEM_OPERATION_TYPE_UNMAP: 2>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemHandleType(value: int)#
Memory handle types
CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_GENERIC = <CUmemHandleType.CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_GENERIC: 0>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemAllocationCompType(value: int)#
Specifies compression attribute for an allocation.
CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_COMP_NONE = <CUmemAllocationCompType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_COMP_NONE: 0>#

Allocating non-compressible memory

CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_COMP_GENERIC = <CUmemAllocationCompType.CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_COMP_GENERIC: 1>#

Allocating compressible memory

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmulticastGranularity_flags(value: int)#
Flags for querying different granularities for a multicast object
CU_MULTICAST_GRANULARITY_MINIMUM = <CUmulticastGranularity_flags.CU_MULTICAST_GRANULARITY_MINIMUM: 0>#

Minimum required granularity

CU_MULTICAST_GRANULARITY_RECOMMENDED = <CUmulticastGranularity_flags.CU_MULTICAST_GRANULARITY_RECOMMENDED: 1>#

Recommended granularity for best performance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphExecUpdateResult(value: int)#
CUDA Graph Update error types
CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_SUCCESS = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_SUCCESS: 0>#

The update succeeded

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR: 1>#

The update failed for an unexpected reason which is described in the return value of the function

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_TOPOLOGY_CHANGED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_TOPOLOGY_CHANGED: 2>#

The update failed because the topology changed

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_NODE_TYPE_CHANGED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_NODE_TYPE_CHANGED: 3>#

The update failed because a node type changed

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_FUNCTION_CHANGED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_FUNCTION_CHANGED: 4>#

The update failed because the function of a kernel node changed (CUDA driver < 11.2)

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_PARAMETERS_CHANGED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_PARAMETERS_CHANGED: 5>#

The update failed because the parameters changed in a way that is not supported

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_NOT_SUPPORTED: 6>#

The update failed because something about the node is not supported

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_FUNCTION_CHANGE = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_UNSUPPORTED_FUNCTION_CHANGE: 7>#

The update failed because the function of a kernel node changed in an unsupported way

CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_ATTRIBUTES_CHANGED = <CUgraphExecUpdateResult.CU_GRAPH_EXEC_UPDATE_ERROR_ATTRIBUTES_CHANGED: 8>#

The update failed because the node attributes changed in a way that is not supported

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemPool_attribute(value: int)#
CUDA memory pool attributes
CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_FOLLOW_EVENT_DEPENDENCIES = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_FOLLOW_EVENT_DEPENDENCIES: 1>#

(value type = int) Allow cuMemAllocAsync to use memory asynchronously freed in another streams as long as a stream ordering dependency of the allocating stream on the free action exists. Cuda events and null stream interactions can create the required stream ordered dependencies. (default enabled)

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_ALLOW_OPPORTUNISTIC = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_ALLOW_OPPORTUNISTIC: 2>#

(value type = int) Allow reuse of already completed frees when there is no dependency between the free and allocation. (default enabled)

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_ALLOW_INTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_REUSE_ALLOW_INTERNAL_DEPENDENCIES: 3>#

(value type = int) Allow cuMemAllocAsync to insert new stream dependencies in order to establish the stream ordering required to reuse a piece of memory released by cuMemFreeAsync (default enabled).

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RELEASE_THRESHOLD = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RELEASE_THRESHOLD: 4>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Amount of reserved memory in bytes to hold onto before trying to release memory back to the OS. When more than the release threshold bytes of memory are held by the memory pool, the allocator will try to release memory back to the OS on the next call to stream, event or context synchronize. (default 0)

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_CURRENT = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_CURRENT: 5>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Amount of backing memory currently allocated for the mempool.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_HIGH = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_HIGH: 6>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) High watermark of backing memory allocated for the mempool since the last time it was reset. High watermark can only be reset to zero.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_USED_MEM_CURRENT = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_USED_MEM_CURRENT: 7>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Amount of memory from the pool that is currently in use by the application.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_USED_MEM_HIGH = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_USED_MEM_HIGH: 8>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) High watermark of the amount of memory from the pool that was in use by the application since the last time it was reset. High watermark can only be reset to zero.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_ALLOCATION_TYPE = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_ALLOCATION_TYPE: 9>#

(value type = CUmemAllocationType) The allocation type of the mempool

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_EXPORT_HANDLE_TYPES = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_EXPORT_HANDLE_TYPES: 10>#

(value type = CUmemAllocationHandleType) Available export handle types for the mempool. For imported pools this value is always CU_MEM_HANDLE_TYPE_NONE as an imported pool cannot be re-exported

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_LOCATION_ID = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_LOCATION_ID: 11>#

(value type = int) The location id for the mempool. If the location type for this pool is CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVISIBLE then ID will be CU_DEVICE_INVALID.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_LOCATION_TYPE = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_LOCATION_TYPE: 12>#

(value type = CUmemLocationType) The location type for the mempool. For imported memory pools where the device is not directly visible to the importing process or pools imported via fabric handles across nodes this will be CU_MEM_LOCATION_TYPE_INVISIBLE.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_MAX_POOL_SIZE = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_MAX_POOL_SIZE: 13>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Maximum size of the pool in bytes, this value may be higher than what was initially passed to cuMemPoolCreate due to alignment requirements. A value of 0 indicates no maximum size. For CU_MEM_ALLOCATION_TYPE_MANAGED and IPC imported pools this value will be system dependent.

CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_HW_DECOMPRESS_ENABLED = <CUmemPool_attribute.CU_MEMPOOL_ATTR_HW_DECOMPRESS_ENABLED: 14>#

(value type = int) Indicates whether the pool has hardware compresssion enabled

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemcpyFlags(value: int)#
Flags to specify for copies within a batch. For more details see
:py:obj:`~.cuMemcpyBatchAsync`.
CU_MEMCPY_FLAG_DEFAULT = <CUmemcpyFlags.CU_MEMCPY_FLAG_DEFAULT: 0>#
CU_MEMCPY_FLAG_PREFER_OVERLAP_WITH_COMPUTE = <CUmemcpyFlags.CU_MEMCPY_FLAG_PREFER_OVERLAP_WITH_COMPUTE: 1>#

Hint to the driver to try and overlap the copy with compute work on the SMs.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder(value: int)#
These flags allow applications to convey the source access ordering
CUDA must maintain. The destination will always be accessed in
stream order.
CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_INVALID = <CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder.CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_INVALID: 0>#

Default invalid.

CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_STREAM = <CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder.CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_STREAM: 1>#

Indicates that access to the source pointer must be in stream order.

CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_DURING_API_CALL = <CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder.CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_DURING_API_CALL: 2>#

Indicates that access to the source pointer can be out of stream order and all accesses must be complete before the API call returns. This flag is suited for ephemeral sources (ex., stack variables) when it’s known that no prior operations in the stream can be accessing the memory and also that the lifetime of the memory is limited to the scope that the source variable was declared in. Specifying this flag allows the driver to optimize the copy and removes the need for the user to synchronize the stream after the API call.

CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_ANY = <CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder.CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_ANY: 3>#

Indicates that access to the source pointer can be out of stream order and the accesses can happen even after the API call returns. This flag is suited for host pointers allocated outside CUDA (ex., via malloc) when it’s known that no prior operations in the stream can be accessing the memory. Specifying this flag allows the driver to optimize the copy on certain platforms.

CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_MAX = <CUmemcpySrcAccessOrder.CU_MEMCPY_SRC_ACCESS_ORDER_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemcpy3DOperandType(value: int)#
These flags allow applications to convey the operand type for
individual copies specified in :py:obj:`~.cuMemcpy3DBatchAsync`.
CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_POINTER = <CUmemcpy3DOperandType.CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_POINTER: 1>#

Memcpy operand is a valid pointer.

CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_ARRAY = <CUmemcpy3DOperandType.CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_ARRAY: 2>#

Memcpy operand is a CUarray.

CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_MAX = <CUmemcpy3DOperandType.CU_MEMCPY_OPERAND_TYPE_MAX: 2147483647>#
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphMem_attribute(value: int)#
CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_USED_MEM_CURRENT = <CUgraphMem_attribute.CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_USED_MEM_CURRENT: 0>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Amount of memory, in bytes, currently associated with graphs

CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_USED_MEM_HIGH = <CUgraphMem_attribute.CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_USED_MEM_HIGH: 1>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) High watermark of memory, in bytes, associated with graphs since the last time it was reset. High watermark can only be reset to zero.

CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_CURRENT = <CUgraphMem_attribute.CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_CURRENT: 2>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) Amount of memory, in bytes, currently allocated for use by the CUDA graphs asynchronous allocator.

CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_HIGH = <CUgraphMem_attribute.CU_GRAPH_MEM_ATTR_RESERVED_MEM_HIGH: 3>#

(value type = cuuint64_t) High watermark of memory, in bytes, currently allocated for use by the CUDA graphs asynchronous allocator.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphChildGraphNodeOwnership(value: int)#
Child graph node ownership
CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_CLONE = <CUgraphChildGraphNodeOwnership.CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_CLONE: 0>#

Default behavior for a child graph node. Child graph is cloned into the parent and memory allocation/free nodes can’t be present in the child graph.

CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_MOVE = <CUgraphChildGraphNodeOwnership.CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_MOVE: 1>#

The child graph is moved to the parent. The handle to the child graph is owned by the parent and will be destroyed when the parent is destroyed.

The following restrictions apply to child graphs after they have been moved: Cannot be independently instantiated or destroyed; Cannot be added as a child graph of a separate parent graph; Cannot be used as an argument to cuGraphExecUpdate; Cannot have additional memory allocation or free nodes added.

CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_INVALID = <CUgraphChildGraphNodeOwnership.CU_GRAPH_CHILD_GRAPH_OWNERSHIP_INVALID: -1>#

Invalid ownership flag. Set when params are queried to prevent accidentally reusing the driver-owned graph object

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesOptions(value: int)#
Bitmasks for
:py:obj:`~.CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_FLUSH_WRITES_OPTIONS`
CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_OPTION_HOST = <CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesOptions.CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_OPTION_HOST: 1>#

cuFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites() and its CUDA Runtime API counterpart are supported on the device.

CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_OPTION_MEMOPS = <CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesOptions.CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_OPTION_MEMOPS: 2>#

The CU_STREAM_WAIT_VALUE_FLUSH flag and the CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES MemOp are supported on the device.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUGPUDirectRDMAWritesOrdering(value: int)#
Platform native ordering for GPUDirect RDMA writes
CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_NONE = <CUGPUDirectRDMAWritesOrdering.CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_NONE: 0>#

The device does not natively support ordering of remote writes. cuFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites() can be leveraged if supported.

CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_OWNER = <CUGPUDirectRDMAWritesOrdering.CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_OWNER: 100>#

Natively, the device can consistently consume remote writes, although other CUDA devices may not.

CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_ALL_DEVICES = <CUGPUDirectRDMAWritesOrdering.CU_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_ORDERING_ALL_DEVICES: 200>#

Any CUDA device in the system can consistently consume remote writes to this device.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesScope(value: int)#
The scopes for :py:obj:`~.cuFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites`
CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TO_OWNER = <CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesScope.CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TO_OWNER: 100>#

Blocks until remote writes are visible to the CUDA device context owning the data.

CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TO_ALL_DEVICES = <CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesScope.CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TO_ALL_DEVICES: 200>#

Blocks until remote writes are visible to all CUDA device contexts.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesTarget(value: int)#
The targets for :py:obj:`~.cuFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites`
CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TARGET_CURRENT_CTX = <CUflushGPUDirectRDMAWritesTarget.CU_FLUSH_GPU_DIRECT_RDMA_WRITES_TARGET_CURRENT_CTX: 0>#

Sets the target for cuFlushGPUDirectRDMAWrites() to the currently active CUDA device context.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphDebugDot_flags(value: int)#
The additional write options for :py:obj:`~.cuGraphDebugDotPrint`
CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_VERBOSE = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_VERBOSE: 1>#

Output all debug data as if every debug flag is enabled

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_RUNTIME_TYPES = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_RUNTIME_TYPES: 2>#

Use CUDA Runtime structures for output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS: 4>#

Adds CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEMCPY_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEMCPY_NODE_PARAMS: 8>#

Adds CUDA_MEMCPY3D values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEMSET_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEMSET_NODE_PARAMS: 16>#

Adds CUDA_MEMSET_NODE_PARAMS values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_HOST_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_HOST_NODE_PARAMS: 32>#

Adds CUDA_HOST_NODE_PARAMS values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EVENT_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EVENT_NODE_PARAMS: 64>#

Adds CUevent handle from record and wait nodes to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXT_SEMAS_SIGNAL_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXT_SEMAS_SIGNAL_NODE_PARAMS: 128>#

Adds CUDA_EXT_SEM_SIGNAL_NODE_PARAMS values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXT_SEMAS_WAIT_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXT_SEMAS_WAIT_NODE_PARAMS: 256>#

Adds CUDA_EXT_SEM_WAIT_NODE_PARAMS values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_KERNEL_NODE_ATTRIBUTES = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_KERNEL_NODE_ATTRIBUTES: 512>#

Adds CUkernelNodeAttrValue values to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_HANDLES = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_HANDLES: 1024>#

Adds node handles and every kernel function handle to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEM_ALLOC_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEM_ALLOC_NODE_PARAMS: 2048>#

Adds memory alloc node parameters to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEM_FREE_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_MEM_FREE_NODE_PARAMS: 4096>#

Adds memory free node parameters to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_BATCH_MEM_OP_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_BATCH_MEM_OP_NODE_PARAMS: 8192>#

Adds batch mem op node parameters to output

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXTRA_TOPO_INFO = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_EXTRA_TOPO_INFO: 16384>#

Adds edge numbering information

CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_CONDITIONAL_NODE_PARAMS = <CUgraphDebugDot_flags.CU_GRAPH_DEBUG_DOT_FLAGS_CONDITIONAL_NODE_PARAMS: 32768>#

Adds conditional node parameters to output

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUuserObject_flags(value: int)#
Flags for user objects for graphs
CU_USER_OBJECT_NO_DESTRUCTOR_SYNC = <CUuserObject_flags.CU_USER_OBJECT_NO_DESTRUCTOR_SYNC: 1>#

Indicates the destructor execution is not synchronized by any CUDA handle.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUuserObjectRetain_flags(value: int)#
Flags for retaining user object references for graphs
CU_GRAPH_USER_OBJECT_MOVE = <CUuserObjectRetain_flags.CU_GRAPH_USER_OBJECT_MOVE: 1>#

Transfer references from the caller rather than creating new references.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphInstantiate_flags(value: int)#
Flags for instantiating a graph
CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_AUTO_FREE_ON_LAUNCH = <CUgraphInstantiate_flags.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_AUTO_FREE_ON_LAUNCH: 1>#

Automatically free memory allocated in a graph before relaunching.

CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_UPLOAD = <CUgraphInstantiate_flags.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_UPLOAD: 2>#

Automatically upload the graph after instantiation. Only supported by cuGraphInstantiateWithParams. The upload will be performed using the stream provided in instantiateParams.

CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_DEVICE_LAUNCH = <CUgraphInstantiate_flags.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_DEVICE_LAUNCH: 4>#

Instantiate the graph to be launchable from the device. This flag can only be used on platforms which support unified addressing. This flag cannot be used in conjunction with CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_AUTO_FREE_ON_LAUNCH.

CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_USE_NODE_PRIORITY = <CUgraphInstantiate_flags.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_FLAG_USE_NODE_PRIORITY: 8>#

Run the graph using the per-node priority attributes rather than the priority of the stream it is launched into.

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUdeviceNumaConfig(value: int)#
CUDA device NUMA configuration
CU_DEVICE_NUMA_CONFIG_NONE = <CUdeviceNumaConfig.CU_DEVICE_NUMA_CONFIG_NONE: 0>#

The GPU is not a NUMA node

CU_DEVICE_NUMA_CONFIG_NUMA_NODE = <CUdeviceNumaConfig.CU_DEVICE_NUMA_CONFIG_NUMA_NODE: 1>#

The GPU is a NUMA node, CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_NUMA_ID contains its NUMA ID

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUprocessState(value: int)#
CUDA Process States
CU_PROCESS_STATE_RUNNING = <CUprocessState.CU_PROCESS_STATE_RUNNING: 0>#

Default process state

CU_PROCESS_STATE_LOCKED = <CUprocessState.CU_PROCESS_STATE_LOCKED: 1>#

CUDA API locks are taken so further CUDA API calls will block

CU_PROCESS_STATE_CHECKPOINTED = <CUprocessState.CU_PROCESS_STATE_CHECKPOINTED: 2>#

Application memory contents have been checkpointed and underlying allocations and device handles have been released

CU_PROCESS_STATE_FAILED = <CUprocessState.CU_PROCESS_STATE_FAILED: 3>#

Application entered an uncorrectable error during the checkpoint/restore process

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUeglFrameType(value: int)#
CUDA EglFrame type - array or pointer
CU_EGL_FRAME_TYPE_ARRAY = <CUeglFrameType.CU_EGL_FRAME_TYPE_ARRAY: 0>#

Frame type CUDA array

CU_EGL_FRAME_TYPE_PITCH = <CUeglFrameType.CU_EGL_FRAME_TYPE_PITCH: 1>#

Frame type pointer

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUeglResourceLocationFlags(value: int)#
Resource location flags- sysmem or vidmem  For CUDA context on
iGPU, since video and system memory are equivalent - these flags
will not have an effect on the execution.  For CUDA context on
dGPU, applications can use the flag
:py:obj:`~.CUeglResourceLocationFlags` to give a hint about the
desired location.  :py:obj:`~.CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_SYSMEM` -
the frame data is made resident on the system memory to be accessed
by CUDA.  :py:obj:`~.CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_VIDMEM` - the frame
data is made resident on the dedicated video memory to be accessed
by CUDA.  There may be an additional latency due to new allocation
and data migration, if the frame is produced on a different memory.
CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_SYSMEM = <CUeglResourceLocationFlags.CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_SYSMEM: 0>#

Resource location sysmem

CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_VIDMEM = <CUeglResourceLocationFlags.CU_EGL_RESOURCE_LOCATION_VIDMEM: 1>#

Resource location vidmem

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUeglColorFormat(value: int)#
CUDA EGL Color Format - The different planar and multiplanar
formats currently supported for CUDA_EGL interops. Three channel
formats are currently not supported for
:py:obj:`~.CU_EGL_FRAME_TYPE_ARRAY`
CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR: 0>#

Y, U, V in three surfaces, each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR: 1>#

Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) with VU byte ordering, width, height ratio same as YUV420Planar.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_PLANAR: 2>#

Y, U, V each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_SEMIPLANAR: 3>#

Y, UV in two surfaces with VU byte ordering, width, height ratio same as YUV422Planar.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RGB: 4>#

R/G/B three channels in one surface with BGR byte ordering. Only pitch linear format supported.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BGR: 5>#

R/G/B three channels in one surface with RGB byte ordering. Only pitch linear format supported.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_ARGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_ARGB: 6>#

R/G/B/A four channels in one surface with BGRA byte ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RGBA = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RGBA: 7>#

R/G/B/A four channels in one surface with ABGR byte ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_L = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_L: 8>#

single luminance channel in one surface.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_R = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_R: 9>#

single color channel in one surface.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_PLANAR: 10>#

Y, U, V in three surfaces, each in a separate surface, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_SEMIPLANAR: 11>#

Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) with VU byte ordering, width, height ratio same as YUV444Planar.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUYV_422 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUYV_422: 12>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as UYVY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_422 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_422: 13>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as YUYV in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_ABGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_ABGR: 14>#

R/G/B/A four channels in one surface with RGBA byte ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BGRA = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BGRA: 15>#

R/G/B/A four channels in one surface with ARGB byte ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_A = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_A: 16>#

Alpha color format - one channel in one surface.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_RG: 17>#

R/G color format - two channels in one surface with GR byte ordering

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_AYUV = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_AYUV: 18>#

Y, U, V, A four channels in one surface, interleaved as VUYA.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_SEMIPLANAR: 19>#

Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_SEMIPLANAR: 20>#

Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR: 21>#

Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR: 22>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR: 23>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR: 24>#

Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR: 25>#

Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_VYUY_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_VYUY_ER: 26>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as YVYU in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_ER: 27>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as YUYV in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUYV_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUYV_ER: 28>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as UYVY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVYU_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVYU_ER: 29>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as VYUY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV_ER: 30>#

Extended Range Y, U, V three channels in one surface, interleaved as VUY. Only pitch linear format supported.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUVA_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUVA_ER: 31>#

Extended Range Y, U, V, A four channels in one surface, interleaved as AVUY.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_AYUV_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_AYUV_ER: 32>#

Extended Range Y, U, V, A four channels in one surface, interleaved as VUYA.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_PLANAR_ER: 33>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in three surfaces, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_PLANAR_ER: 34>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in three surfaces, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_ER: 35>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in three surfaces, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV444_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 36>#

Extended Range Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) with VU byte ordering, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV422_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 37>#

Extended Range Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) with VU byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 38>#

Extended Range Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) with VU byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_PLANAR_ER: 39>#

Extended Range Y, V, U in three surfaces, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_PLANAR_ER: 40>#

Extended Range Y, V, U in three surfaces, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_ER: 41>#

Extended Range Y, V, U in three surfaces, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 42>#

Extended Range Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 43>#

Extended Range Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 44>#

Extended Range Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) with UV byte ordering, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_RGGB: 45>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_BGGR: 46>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_GRBG: 47>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_GBRG: 48>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_RGGB: 49>#

Bayer10 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering. Out of 16 bits, 10 bits used 6 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_BGGR: 50>#

Bayer10 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering. Out of 16 bits, 10 bits used 6 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_GRBG: 51>#

Bayer10 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 10 bits used 6 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_GBRG: 52>#

Bayer10 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 10 bits used 6 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_RGGB: 53>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_BGGR: 54>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_GRBG: 55>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_GBRG: 56>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_RGGB: 57>#

Bayer14 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering. Out of 16 bits, 14 bits used 2 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_BGGR: 58>#

Bayer14 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering. Out of 16 bits, 14 bits used 2 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_GRBG: 59>#

Bayer14 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 14 bits used 2 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER14_GBRG: 60>#

Bayer14 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering. Out of 16 bits, 14 bits used 2 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_RGGB: 61>#

Bayer20 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering. Out of 32 bits, 20 bits used 12 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_BGGR: 62>#

Bayer20 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering. Out of 32 bits, 20 bits used 12 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_GRBG: 63>#

Bayer20 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering. Out of 32 bits, 20 bits used 12 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER20_GBRG: 64>#

Bayer20 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering. Out of 32 bits, 20 bits used 12 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU444_PLANAR: 65>#

Y, V, U in three surfaces, each in a separate surface, U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU422_PLANAR: 66>#

Y, V, U in three surfaces, each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR: 67>#

Y, V, U in three surfaces, each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_RGGB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_RGGB: 68>#

Nvidia proprietary Bayer ISP format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RGGB ordering and mapped to opaque integer datatype.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_BGGR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_BGGR: 69>#

Nvidia proprietary Bayer ISP format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BGGR ordering and mapped to opaque integer datatype.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_GRBG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_GRBG: 70>#

Nvidia proprietary Bayer ISP format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GRBG ordering and mapped to opaque integer datatype.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_GBRG = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_ISP_GBRG: 71>#

Nvidia proprietary Bayer ISP format - one channel in one surface with interleaved GBRG ordering and mapped to opaque integer datatype.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_BCCR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_BCCR: 72>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BCCR ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_RCCB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_RCCB: 73>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RCCB ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_CRBC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_CRBC: 74>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CRBC ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_CBRC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER_CBRC: 75>#

Bayer format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CBRC ordering.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_CCCC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER10_CCCC: 76>#

Bayer10 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CCCC ordering. Out of 16 bits, 10 bits used 6 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_BCCR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_BCCR: 77>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved BCCR ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_RCCB = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_RCCB: 78>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved RCCB ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CRBC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CRBC: 79>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CRBC ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CBRC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CBRC: 80>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CBRC ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CCCC = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_BAYER12_CCCC: 81>#

Bayer12 format - one channel in one surface with interleaved CCCC ordering. Out of 16 bits, 12 bits used 4 bits No-op.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y: 82>#

Color format for single Y plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_2020: 83>#

Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_2020: 84>#

Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_2020: 85>#

Y, U, V each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height= 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_2020: 86>#

Y, V, U each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_SEMIPLANAR_709: 87>#

Y, UV in two surfaces (UV as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_SEMIPLANAR_709: 88>#

Y, VU in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV420_PLANAR_709: 89>#

Y, U, V each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVU420_PLANAR_709: 90>#

Y, V, U each in a separate surface, U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_709: 91>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface), U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_2020: 92>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface), U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR_2020: 93>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces(VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR: 94>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces(VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_422_SEMIPLANAR_709: 95>#

Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces(VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y_ER: 96>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y_709_ER: 97>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10_ER: 98>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y10 plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10_709_ER: 99>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y10 plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12_ER: 100>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y12 plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12_709_ER: 101>#

Extended Range Color format for single Y12 plane.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUVA = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUVA: 102>#

Y, U, V, A four channels in one surface, interleaved as AVUY.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YUV: 103>#

Y, U, V three channels in one surface, interleaved as VUY. Only pitch linear format supported.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVYU = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_YVYU: 104>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as YVYU in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_VYUY = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_VYUY: 105>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as VYUY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 106>#

Extended Range Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces(VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_420_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER: 107>#

Extended Range Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces(VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 108>#

Extended Range Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y10V10U10_444_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER: 109>#

Extended Range Y10, V10U10 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 110>#

Extended Range Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_420_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER: 111>#

Extended Range Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = 1/2 Y width, U/V height = 1/2 Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR_ER: 112>#

Extended Range Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_Y12V12U12_444_SEMIPLANAR_709_ER: 113>#

Extended Range Y12, V12U12 in two surfaces (VU as one surface) U/V width = Y width, U/V height = Y height.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_709 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_709: 114>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as UYVY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_709_ER = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_709_ER: 115>#

Extended Range Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as UYVY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_2020 = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_UYVY_2020: 116>#

Y, U, V in one surface, interleaved as UYVY in one channel.

CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_MAX = <CUeglColorFormat.CU_EGL_COLOR_FORMAT_MAX: 117>#

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUmemFabricHandle_v1#
Fabric handle - An opaque handle representing a memory allocation
that can be exported to processes in same or different nodes. For
IPC between processes on different nodes they must be connected via
the NVSwitch fabric.

.. attribute:: data



   :type: bytes

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUstreamBatchMemOpParams_v1#
Per-operation parameters for cuStreamBatchMemOp

.. attribute:: operation

   Operation. This is the first field of all the union elemets and
   acts as a TAG to determine which union member is valid.

   :type: CUstreamBatchMemOpType

.. attribute:: waitValue

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_32 and
   CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WAIT_VALUE_64 operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpWaitValueParams_st

.. attribute:: writeValue

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_32 and
   CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_WRITE_VALUE_64 operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpWriteValueParams_st

.. attribute:: flushRemoteWrites

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_FLUSH_REMOTE_WRITES operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpFlushRemoteWritesParams_st

.. attribute:: memoryBarrier

   Params for CU_STREAM_MEM_OP_BARRIER operations.

   :type: CUstreamMemOpMemoryBarrierParams_st

.. attribute:: atomicReduction



   :type: CUstreamMemOpAtomicReductionParams_st

.. attribute:: pad



   :type: list[cuuint64_t]

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

.. attribute:: maxGridSize

.. attribute:: sharedMemPerBlock

   Shared memory available per block in bytes

   :type: int

.. attribute:: totalConstantMemory

   Constant memory available on device in bytes

   :type: int

.. attribute:: SIMDWidth

   Warp size in threads

   :type: int

.. attribute:: memPitch

   Maximum pitch in bytes allowed by memory copies

   :type: int

.. attribute:: regsPerBlock

   32-bit registers available per block

   :type: int

.. attribute:: clockRate

   Clock frequency in kilohertz

   :type: int

.. attribute:: textureAlign

   Alignment requirement for textures

   :type: int

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUaccessPolicyWindow_v1#
Specifies an access policy for a window, a contiguous extent of
memory beginning at base_ptr and ending at base_ptr + num_bytes.
num_bytes is limited by
CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAX_ACCESS_POLICY_WINDOW_SIZE. Partition into
many segments and assign segments such that: sum of "hit segments"
/ window == approx. ratio. sum of "miss segments" / window ==
approx 1-ratio. Segments and ratio specifications are fitted to the
capabilities of the architecture. Accesses in a hit segment apply
the hitProp access policy. Accesses in a miss segment apply the
missProp access policy.

.. attribute:: base_ptr

   Starting address of the access policy window. CUDA driver may align
   it.

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: num_bytes

   Size in bytes of the window policy. CUDA driver may restrict the
   maximum size and alignment.

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: hitRatio

   hitRatio specifies percentage of lines assigned hitProp, rest are
   assigned missProp.

   :type: float

.. attribute:: hitProp

   CUaccessProperty set for hit.

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. attribute:: missProp

   CUaccessProperty set for miss. Must be either NORMAL or STREAMING

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUaccessPolicyWindow#
Specifies an access policy for a window, a contiguous extent of
memory beginning at base_ptr and ending at base_ptr + num_bytes.
num_bytes is limited by
CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MAX_ACCESS_POLICY_WINDOW_SIZE. Partition into
many segments and assign segments such that: sum of "hit segments"
/ window == approx. ratio. sum of "miss segments" / window ==
approx 1-ratio. Segments and ratio specifications are fitted to the
capabilities of the architecture. Accesses in a hit segment apply
the hitProp access policy. Accesses in a miss segment apply the
missProp access policy.

.. attribute:: base_ptr

   Starting address of the access policy window. CUDA driver may align
   it.

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: num_bytes

   Size in bytes of the window policy. CUDA driver may restrict the
   maximum size and alignment.

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: hitRatio

   hitRatio specifies percentage of lines assigned hitProp, rest are
   assigned missProp.

   :type: float

.. attribute:: hitProp

   CUaccessProperty set for hit.

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. attribute:: missProp

   CUaccessProperty set for miss. Must be either NORMAL or STREAMING

   :type: CUaccessProperty

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS_v1#
GPU kernel node parameters

.. attribute:: func

   Kernel to launch

   :type: CUfunction

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: kernelParams

   Array of pointers to kernel parameters

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: extra

   Extra options

   :type: Any

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_KERNEL_NODE_PARAMS_v2#
GPU kernel node parameters

.. attribute:: func

   Kernel to launch

   :type: CUfunction

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: kernelParams

   Array of pointers to kernel parameters

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: extra

   Extra options

   :type: Any

.. attribute:: kern

   Kernel to launch, will only be referenced if func is NULL

   :type: CUkernel

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context for the kernel task to run in. The value NULL will indicate
   the current context should be used by the api. This field is
   ignored if func is set.

   :type: CUcontext

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_MEMSET_NODE_PARAMS_v1#
Memset node parameters

.. attribute:: dst

   Destination device pointer

   :type: CUdeviceptr

.. attribute:: pitch

   Pitch of destination device pointer. Unused if height is 1

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: value

   Value to be set

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: elementSize

   Size of each element in bytes. Must be 1, 2, or 4.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: width

   Width of the row in elements

   :type: size_t

.. attribute:: height

   Number of rows

   :type: size_t

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_CONDITIONAL_NODE_PARAMS(void_ptr _ptr=0)#
Conditional node parameters

.. attribute:: handle

   Conditional node handle. Handles must be created in advance of
   creating the node using cuGraphConditionalHandleCreate.

   :type: CUgraphConditionalHandle

.. attribute:: type

   Type of conditional node.

   :type: CUgraphConditionalNodeType

.. attribute:: size

   Size of graph output array. Allowed values are 1 for
   CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_WHILE, 1 or 2 for CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_IF, or any
   value greater than zero for CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_SWITCH.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: phGraph_out

   CUDA-owned array populated with conditional node child graphs
   during creation of the node. Valid for the lifetime of the
   conditional node. The contents of the graph(s) are subject to the
   following constraints:   - Allowed node types are kernel nodes,
   empty nodes, child graphs, memsets, memcopies, and conditionals.
   This applies recursively to child graphs and conditional bodies.
   - All kernels, including kernels in nested conditionals or child
   graphs at any level, must belong to the same CUDA context.
   These graphs may be populated using graph node creation APIs or
   cuStreamBeginCaptureToGraph.  CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_IF: phGraph_out[0]
   is executed when the condition is non-zero. If `size` == 2,
   phGraph_out[1] will be executed when the condition is zero.
   CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_WHILE: phGraph_out[0] is executed as long as the
   condition is non-zero. CU_GRAPH_COND_TYPE_SWITCH: phGraph_out[n] is
   executed when the condition is equal to n. If the condition >=
   `size`, no body graph is executed.

   :type: CUgraph

.. attribute:: ctx

   Context on which to run the node. Must match context used to create
   the handle and all body nodes.

   :type: CUcontext

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUgraphEdgeData#
Optional annotation for edges in a CUDA graph. Note, all edges
implicitly have annotations and default to a zero-initialized value
if not specified. A zero-initialized struct indicates a standard
full serialization of two nodes with memory visibility.

.. attribute:: from_port

   This indicates when the dependency is triggered from the upstream
   node on the edge. The meaning is specfic to the node type. A value
   of 0 in all cases means full completion of the upstream node, with
   memory visibility to the downstream node or portion thereof
   (indicated by `to_port`).   Only kernel nodes define non-zero
   ports. A kernel node can use the following output port types:
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_DEFAULT,
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_PROGRAMMATIC, or
   CU_GRAPH_KERNEL_NODE_PORT_LAUNCH_ORDER.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: to_port

   This indicates what portion of the downstream node is dependent on
   the upstream node or portion thereof (indicated by `from_port`).
   The meaning is specific to the node type. A value of 0 in all cases
   means the entirety of the downstream node is dependent on the
   upstream work.   Currently no node types define non-zero ports.
   Accordingly, this field must be set to zero.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: type

   This should be populated with a value from CUgraphDependencyType.
   (It is typed as char due to compiler-specific layout of bitfields.)
   See CUgraphDependencyType.

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: reserved

   These bytes are unused and must be zeroed. This ensures
   compatibility if additional fields are added in the future.

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUDA_GRAPH_INSTANTIATE_PARAMS#
Graph instantiation parameters

.. attribute:: flags

   Instantiation flags

   :type: cuuint64_t

.. attribute:: hUploadStream

   Upload stream

   :type: CUstream

.. attribute:: hErrNode_out

   The node which caused instantiation to fail, if any

   :type: CUgraphNode

.. attribute:: result_out

   Whether instantiation was successful. If it failed, the reason why

   :type: CUgraphInstantiateResult

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap#
Memory Synchronization Domain map  See ::cudaLaunchMemSyncDomain.
By default, kernels are launched in domain 0. Kernel launched with
CU_LAUNCH_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_REMOTE will have a different domain ID.
User may also alter the domain ID with CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap for
a specific stream / graph node / kernel launch. See
CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_MAP.  Domain ID range is
available through CU_DEVICE_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_COUNT.

.. attribute:: default_

   The default domain ID to use for designated kernels

   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: remote

   The remote domain ID to use for designated kernels

   :type: bytes

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchAttributeValue#
Launch attributes union; used as value field of CUlaunchAttribute

.. attribute:: pad



   :type: bytes

.. attribute:: accessPolicyWindow

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_ACCESS_POLICY_WINDOW.

   :type: CUaccessPolicyWindow

.. attribute:: cooperative

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_COOPERATIVE. Nonzero
   indicates a cooperative kernel (see cuLaunchCooperativeKernel).

   :type: int

.. attribute:: syncPolicy

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_SYNCHRONIZATION_POLICY. CUsynchronizationPolicy
   for work queued up in this stream

   :type: CUsynchronizationPolicy

.. attribute:: clusterDim

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION
   that represents the desired cluster dimensions for the kernel.
   Opaque type with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of
   the cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension.
   - `y` - The Y dimension of the cluster, in blocks. Must be a
   divisor of the grid Y dimension.    - `z` - The Z dimension of the
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid Z dimension.

   :type: anon_struct1

.. attribute:: clusterSchedulingPolicyPreference

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_SCHEDULING_POLICY_PREFERENCE. Cluster
   scheduling policy preference for the kernel.

   :type: CUclusterSchedulingPolicy

.. attribute:: programmaticStreamSerializationAllowed

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PROGRAMMATIC_STREAM_SERIALIZATION.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: programmaticEvent

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PROGRAMMATIC_EVENT
   with the following fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when
   all blocks trigger it.    - `Event` record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept :CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.
   - `triggerAtBlockStart` - If this is set to non-0, each block
   launch will automatically trigger the event.

   :type: anon_struct2

.. attribute:: launchCompletionEvent

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_LAUNCH_COMPLETION_EVENT with the following
   fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when the last block
   launches    - `int` flags; - Event record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.

   :type: anon_struct3

.. attribute:: priority

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PRIORITY. Execution
   priority of the kernel.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: memSyncDomainMap

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_MAP.
   See CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap.

   :type: CUlaunchMemSyncDomainMap

.. attribute:: memSyncDomain

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN.
   See::CUlaunchMemSyncDomain

   :type: CUlaunchMemSyncDomain

.. attribute:: preferredClusterDim

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_CLUSTER_DIMENSION that represents the
   desired preferred cluster dimensions for the kernel. Opaque type
   with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of the preferred
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension, and
   must be a multiple of the `x` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `y` - The Y dimension of
   the preferred cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid Y
   dimension, and must be a multiple of the `y` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `z` - The Z dimension of
   the preferred cluster, in blocks. Must be equal to the `z` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.

   :type: anon_struct4

.. attribute:: deviceUpdatableKernelNode

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_DEVICE_UPDATABLE_KERNEL_NODE. with the
   following fields: - `int` deviceUpdatable - Whether or not the
   resulting kernel node should be device-updatable.    -
   `CUgraphDeviceNode` devNode - Returns a handle to pass to the
   various device-side update functions.

   :type: anon_struct5

.. attribute:: sharedMemCarveout

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_SHARED_MEMORY_CARVEOUT.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: nvlinkUtilCentricScheduling



   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: portableClusterSizeMode

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PORTABLE_CLUSTER_SIZE_MODE.

   :type: CUlaunchAttributePortableClusterMode

.. attribute:: sharedMemoryMode

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_SHARED_MEMORY_MODE.
   See CUsharedMemoryMode for acceptable values.

   :type: CUsharedMemoryMode

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchAttribute#
Launch attribute

.. attribute:: id

   Attribute to set

   :type: CUlaunchAttributeID

.. attribute:: value

   Value of the attribute

   :type: CUlaunchAttributeValue

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance
class cuda.bindings.driver.CUlaunchConfig#
CUDA extensible launch configuration

.. attribute:: gridDimX

   Width of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimY

   Height of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: gridDimZ

   Depth of grid in blocks

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimX

   X dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimY

   Y dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: blockDimZ

   Z dimension of each thread block

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: sharedMemBytes

   Dynamic shared-memory size per thread block in bytes

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: hStream

   Stream identifier

   :type: CUstream

.. attribute:: attrs

   List of attributes; nullable if CUlaunchConfig::numAttrs == 0

   :type: CUlaunchAttribute

.. attribute:: numAttrs

   Number of attributes populated in CUlaunchConfig::attrs

   :type: unsigned int

.. method:: getPtr()

   Get memory address of class instance

   :type: int

.. attribute:: programmaticEvent

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PROGRAMMATIC_EVENT
   with the following fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when

   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept :CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.
   - `triggerAtBlockStart` - If this is set to non-0, each block

.. attribute:: launchCompletionEvent

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_LAUNCH_COMPLETION_EVENT with the following

   launches    - `int` flags; - Event record flags, see

   :type: anon_struct3

.. attribute:: priority

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PRIORITY. Execution

   :type: int

.. attribute:: memSyncDomainMap

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN_MAP.

.. attribute:: memSyncDomain

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_MEM_SYNC_DOMAIN.

   :type: CUlaunchMemSyncDomain

.. attribute:: preferredClusterDim

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_CLUSTER_DIMENSION that represents the
   desired preferred cluster dimensions for the kernel. Opaque type
   with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of the preferred
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension, and
   must be a multiple of the `x` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `y` - The Y dimension of

   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.    - `z` - The Z dimension of
   the preferred cluster, in blocks. Must be equal to the `z` field of
   CUlaunchAttributeValue::clusterDim.

   :type: anon_struct4

.. attribute:: deviceUpdatableKernelNode

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_DEVICE_UPDATABLE_KERNEL_NODE. with the
   following fields: - `int` deviceUpdatable - Whether or not the
   resulting kernel node should be device-updatable.    -
   `CUgraphDeviceNode` devNode - Returns a handle to pass to the
   various device-side update functions.

   :type: anon_struct5

.. attribute:: sharedMemCarveout

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_SHARED_MEMORY_CARVEOUT.

   :type: unsigned int

.. attribute:: nvlinkUtilCentricScheduling

.. attribute:: clusterDim

   Value of launch attribute CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_CLUSTER_DIMENSION
   that represents the desired cluster dimensions for the kernel.
   Opaque type with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of
   the cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension.
   - `y` - The Y dimension of the cluster, in blocks. Must be a
   divisor of the grid Y dimension.    - `z` - The Z dimension of the

.. attribute:: programmaticEvent

   with the following fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when
   all blocks trigger it.    - `Event` record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept :CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.
   - `triggerAtBlockStart` - If this is set to non-0, each block
   launch will automatically trigger the event.

   :type: anon_struct2

.. attribute:: launchCompletionEvent

   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_LAUNCH_COMPLETION_EVENT with the following
   fields: - `CUevent` event - Event to fire when the last block
   launches    - `int` flags; - Event record flags, see
   cuEventRecordWithFlags. Does not accept CU_EVENT_RECORD_EXTERNAL.

   :type: anon_struct3

.. attribute:: priority

   priority of the kernel.

   :type: int

.. attribute:: preferredClusterDim

   Value of launch attribute
   CU_LAUNCH_ATTRIBUTE_PREFERRED_CLUSTER_DIMENSION that represents the

   with the following fields: - `x` - The X dimension of the preferred
   cluster, in blocks. Must be a divisor of the grid X dimension, and
   must be a multiple of the `x` field of
